layers.title: Implement log resolution
layers.summary: This layer adds the new logs command implementation. It validates the requested worker address and line count, resolves the matching worker process through the existing -worker_address signal, derives the INFO log path from process flags or worker config defaults, and shells out to tail to print the requested number of lines.
layers.diff:
+class Command(BaseCommand):
+    """Print the last N lines of a worker's log file to stdout."""
+
+    name = "logs"
+    description = "print recent log lines for a worker service"
+
+    def add_arguments(self, parser):
+        """Add ``logs`` arguments to the parser."""
+        parser.add_argument(
+            "-w", "--worker_address", metavar="ADDR", required=True,
+            help="address (host:port) of the worker whose log to print",
+        )
+        parser.add_argument(
+            "-n", "--lines", metavar="N", default=_DEFAULT_LINES, type=_parse_positive_int,
+            help="number of trailing log lines to print",
+        )
+
+    def run(self, args):
+        """Print the tail of the worker's log to stdout."""
+        try:
+            address = _validate_worker_address(args.worker_address)
+            lines = _parse_positive_int(args.lines)
+        except (ValueError, argparse.ArgumentTypeError) as exc:
+            self.logger.error(str(exc))
+            return BaseCommand.FAILURE

layers.title: Register logs subcommand
layers.summary: This layer exposes the implementation through the existing dscli command module list. Relative to the implementation layer, it is the CLI routing hook that makes dscli discover and dispatch the new logs subcommand.

layers.title: Cover command behavior
layers.summary: This layer adds unit coverage around the CLI behavior introduced by the first two layers. The tests verify default and process-specified log path selection, successful tail invocation, and rejection of malformed worker addresses or line counts before tail is run.
layers.diff:
+class TestLogsCommand(unittest.TestCase):
+    """Tests for :class:`yr.datasystem.cli.logs.Command`."""
+
+    @mock.patch.object(logs.Command, "load_worker_config_defaults")
+    @mock.patch.object(
+        logs.Command,
+        "get_process_args",
+        return_value=["/opt/yuanrong/datasystem/bin/datasystem_worker"],
+    )
+    @mock.patch("yr.datasystem.cli.logs.subprocess.run")
+    @mock.patch.object(logs.Command, "find_worker_pid", return_value=4321)
+    def test_run_prints_recent_lines_from_default_worker_info_log(
+        self, _mock_pid, mock_run, _mock_args, mock_defaults
+    ):
+        """A running worker's log tail is printed and the command succeeds."""
+        mock_defaults.return_value = {"log_dir": "./datasystem/logs", "log_filename": ""}
+        mock_run.return_value = mock.Mock(returncode=0, stdout="a\nb\nc\n", stderr="")

layers.title: Document design plan
layers.summary: This final layer records the intended operator-facing behavior and implementation steps. It describes the local read-only scope, the worker-address discovery model, command registration, and the expected verification path for the new dscli logs command.
layers.diff:
+## Summary
+
+Add a read-only `dscli logs` subcommand that prints the last N lines of a
+datasystem worker's log file, selected by the worker's address. This fills the
+gap between `start`/`stop` and operators falling back to locating log files by
+hand.
+
+## Scope
+
+**In scope:**
+- A `logs` subcommand: `dscli logs -w <host:port> [-n N]`.
+- Output: the last N lines of the worker's log on stdout (default N = 100).
+- Local host only; read-only (no mutation of worker state).

summary: This PR adds a read-only dscli logs subcommand for tailing a local worker INFO log by worker address, with registration, unit tests, and design documentation.
